finding: Banks' willingness to lend stayed positive in Slovakia but eased
bullets: A net 7.8% of Slovak firms (n=306) reported that banks' willingness to provide credit improved, easing 2.6 pp from a net 10.4% in the previous wave, yet remaining above the EA's net 5.5% — suggesting a still-favourable but weakening credit supply environment for firms.

Access to public financial support deteriorated notably for a net 33.8% of Slovak firms (n=298), worsening by 8.8 pp from a net 25.0% in the prior wave, far exceeding the EA's broadly unchanged net 15.5% — putting pressure on firms reliant on state-backed financing.
The general economic outlook deteriorated further for a net 44.4% of Slovak firms (n=442), worsening by 6.1 pp from a net 38.3% — 18.3 pp more adverse than the EA's net 26.1%.
chart_subtitle: Bank willingness to lend remained positive at net 7.8% in Slovakia.
